nodes.label: AI as assistant for complex work
nodes.label_negative: AI as encroaching on creative core
nodes.grounding_span: the more tedious and less creative parts of running an art business

nodes.label: AI as brainstorming and idea generator
nodes.label_negative: AI as replacement for human creativity
nodes.grounding_span: I ask AI to lean into a certain voice... I can glean some ideas

nodes.label: AI environmental impact
nodes.grounding_span: the use of AI does require a lot of environmental resources

nodes.label: AI as threat to artistic endeavors
nodes.grounding_span: AI-generated art will have a huge effect on my field

nodes.label: AI as supplement to creative process
nodes.grounding_span: AI-generated images being used as reference material when it might contain glaring inaccuracies

nodes.label: Creative autonomy
nodes.grounding_span: I prefer to use my own natural brain to come up with illustration ideas

nodes.label: Environmental responsibility
nodes.grounding_span: I'm very much concerned about climate change and its effects

nodes.label: Professional efficiency and brand cohesion
nodes.grounding_span: perfecting my branding... it's all cohesive

nodes.label: Positive acceptance of AI for business tasks
nodes.grounding_span: it's been a super helpful tool to make my website, my social media, and my business plan look and feel exactly how I want it to

nodes.label: Concern about AI environmental impact
nodes.grounding_span: I do sometimes feel bad using it for quick jobs

nodes.label: Concern for artists directly impacted by AI
nodes.grounding_span: I am very concerned that AI-generated art will have a huge effect on my field

nodes.label: Resistance to AI in creative work
nodes.grounding_span: I am also concerned about AI-generated images being used as reference material when it might contain glaring inaccuracies

nodes.label: Conditional optimism if environmental impact is addressed
nodes.grounding_span: I hope in the near future, these environmental concerns can be addressed

nodes.label: Domain-based boundary setting — clear separation of AI-appropriate vs. human-only tasks
nodes.grounding_span: I have a pretty clear idea of how I want my 'brand' to look... I almost exclusively use large language models rather than image-generating models

nodes.label: Ethical and environmental consideration in tool adoption
nodes.grounding_span: I understand that the use of AI does require a lot of environmental resources... I do sometimes feel bad using it for quick jobs
